summary: Iteration 129: Tested Supplier Credit Status Dynamic Computation Bug Fix. All 10 backend API tests passed (100%). Frontend verified - supplier cards correctly show dynamically computed current_credit and total_purchases. The fix works: GET /suppliers now computes current_credit = max(0, credit_expenses - cash/bank_payments) using MongoDB aggregation pipelines.

minor.issue: current_outstanding field reads from stored supplier.current_credit instead of computing dynamically. This shows stale data inconsistent with GET /suppliers. Low priority - ledger primarily shows transaction history.

action_items: OPTIONAL: Update ledger endpoint (/api/suppliers/{id}/ledger) to also compute current_outstanding dynamically for consistency
critical_code_review_comments: GET /suppliers correctly uses MongoDB aggregation pipelines for performance
Dynamic credit formula: max(0, credit_expenses - cash/bank_payments) is correct
Frontend correctly displays current_credit and total_purchases from API response
seed_data_creation: Created test supplier TEST_CreditCheck_* with expenses and payments, cleaned up after tests
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 129: Verified dynamic credit computation in GET /suppliers. The endpoint now uses MongoDB aggregation pipelines to compute current_credit as (total_credit_mode_expenses - total_cash_bank_supplier_payments). Supplier cards show correct values. Test created at /app/backend/tests/test_iter129_supplier_credit_dynamic.py covers: initial credit=0, credit expense increases credit, cash/bank payments decrease credit, full flow calculation, ledger verification, cash expense doesn't increase credit.

get_suppliers_dynamic_credit.test_case: current_credit computed dynamically, not from stored value
get_suppliers_dynamic_credit.details: Returns correct credit = max(0, credit_expenses - cash/bank_payments)

credit_expense_increases_credit.test_case: POST /api/expenses with payment_mode=credit increases supplier credit

cash_payment_decreases_credit.details: 200 cash payment -> credit reduced from 500 to 300

bank_payment_decreases_credit.test_case: Bank payment also reduces credit balance
bank_payment_decreases_credit.details: 100 bank payment -> credit reduced from 300 to 200

full_flow_calculation.test_case: Total credit bills (800) - Total payments (300) = 500 credit
full_flow_calculation.details: Dynamic computation verified across multiple transactions

total_purchases_correct.test_case: total_purchases includes all expenses for supplier
total_purchases_correct.details: Returns sum of all expenses regardless of payment_mode

frontend_card_display.test_case: Supplier cards display Credit Status and Total Purchases correctly
frontend_card_display.details: Cards show SAR values matching API response

frontend_ledger_dialog.test_case: View Ledger shows transaction history with credit/payments breakdown
frontend_ledger_dialog.details: Dialog opens with Current Credit Balance, Credit Purchases, Cash/Bank Purchases, Credit Paid sections

tests: Credit Status displayed on cards - PASS
Total Purchases displayed on cards - PASS
View Ledger button opens dialog - PASS
Ledger dialog shows credit breakdown - PASS

rca_of_the_issue.original_bug: Supplier credit status not updating on the card display but showing correctly in the ledger
rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: GET /suppliers was returning the stale stored 'current_credit' field from the supplier document instead of computing it dynamically
rca_of_the_issue.fix_applied: GET /suppliers now uses MongoDB aggregation pipelines to compute current_credit = max(0, sum(credit_expenses) - sum(cash/bank_payments))
rca_of_the_issue.verification: All tests pass - credit updates immediately after adding expenses or payments
